intro: My research journey has focused on applying deep learning to challenging computer vision problems across multiple domains. Through internships at IISc and NITK, I have worked on multi-object tracking, remote sensing, and medical imaging, gaining experience in experimentation, benchmarking, model evaluation, and system-level analysis.

entries.description: Research on multi-object tracking for traffic surveillance.
highlights: Investigated online and offline multi-object tracking methods for traffic surveillance, implementing and evaluating multiple tracking algorithms to understand identity preservation, track association, and failure modes in real-world CCTV data.

Contributing to an in-house traffic analytics platform at IISc, testing detection and tracking pipelines, debugging model failures, and making incremental improvements to enhance tracking consistency and system reliability.

entries.role: Research Intern — Deep Learning and Computer Vision Lab
entries.description: Dual-domain deep learning research in Remote Sensing and Medical Imaging.
highlights: Conducted dual-domain deep learning research in Remote Sensing and Medical Imaging, leading to a first-author manuscript submission to IEEE J-STARS.
Developed a controlled benchmarking framework for high-resolution building change detection on LEVIR-CD and EGY-BCD datasets, managing preprocessing for 6,000+ image pairs.
Evaluated 8 widely used change detection architectures under a standardized evaluation framework to build consistent performance baselines.

entries.organization: AIQuantum Smart Solutions Pvt. Ltd., NITK STEP Incubator
entries.description: Semantic segmentation for aerial imagery and foundational deep learning work.
highlights: Implemented attention-augmented U-Net variants for road segmentation on aerial satellite imagery.
Reimplemented convolution, pooling, and activation layers from scratch in NumPy to validate gradient propagation and numerical stability against framework outputs.
